data.title: TransAlta surges as Alberta touts $100B plan to attract AI data centers
data.description:
TransAlta (NYSE:TAC) +5.6% in Thursday's trading after Alberta's provincial government unveiled a strategy to become a hub for artificial intelligence data centers, seeing the potential to attract C$100B in investments within five years.

Alberta Technology and Innovation Minister Nate Glubish said he has made four trips to Silicon Valley to meet officials at Apple, Alphabet and Meta Platforms on building data centers in the province.

The strategy seeks to take advantage of Alberta's abundance of natural gas for power generation, low taxes and cold climate, all of which could reduce cooling costs for the companies.

The government will clarify rules for grid-connected infrastructure early next year, Glubish said.

"Potential power supply proponents will welcome additional clarity on the path forward for grid-connected projects that is now expected in early 2025," TD Cowen analyst John Mould says, according to Bloomberg, pointing to TransAlta (TAC) and Capital Power (OTCPK:CPXWF) as potential beneficiaries.

data.title: PayPal said to boost some merchant fees in user agreement amendments
data.description:
Barclays analyst Ramsey El-Assal highlighted changes in PayPal Holdings' (NASDAQ:PYPL) user agreement that boosts pricing applicable to merchants for four of its products — PayPal Pay Later, Alternative Payment Methods, Advanced Credit/Debit Payments, and Virtual Terminal.

The changes take effect on Jan. 13, 2025, after the holiday spending season.

The changes, as described by El-Assal:

  • PayPal Pay Later: 4.99% + fixed fee of US$0.49 or EUR 0.39; previously, merchants were charged the standard PayPal Checkout fee of 3.49% + a fixed fee of US$0.49 or EUR 0.39.
  • Alternative Payment and Advanced Credit/Debit: 2.89% + fixed fee of US$0.29 or EUR 0.19 vs. prior fees of 2.59% + fixed fee of US$0.49 or EUR 0.39.
  • Virtual Terminal: 3.39% + fixed fee of US$0.29 or EUR 0.19 vs. prior schedule of 3.09% + fixed fee of US$0.49 or EUR 0.39.

PayPal (NASDAQ:PYPL) stock slipped 0.7% in Thursday morning trading.

data.title: Blink Charging partners with Power Design to provide 429 charging stations
data.description:
• Blink Charging Co. (NASDAQ:BLNK) has been selected by Power Design, to provide EV chargers and charging services at select locations.
  • The new agreement includes the provision of 429 Blink chargers, with a total of 723 plugs for use by residents who will be living and working within Power Design projects.
  • Power Design will own the Blink charging stations, and when installed, the owners/property managers of the projects will claim ownership.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Meta working to move half of MR glasses production to Vietnam and outsource hardware design
data.description:
Tech giant Meta (NASDAQ:META) is relocating part of the development and design of its mixed reality headsets to Chinese contract manufacturer Goertek, The Information reported Thursday, citing a Goertek employee and two employees at Meta. The move is expected to prevent deep financial losses and navigate a possible trade war between the U.S. and China.

The report, citing a Meta employee, said the company is working with Goertek to move up to half the manufacturing of Quest headsets to Vietnam from China over the next several years. A Goertek employee said the company had already been planning to move some production of Quest headsets to Vietnam, as U.S.-China tensions escalated.

Meta wants to shift more of the design of components such as lenses and displays to Goertek over time, the report said. According to one of the Meta employees, the company hopes Goertek and other potential manufacturers will fully develop its mixed reality headsets as original design manufacturers by 2030. This will allow the company to deepen its focus on the more profitable software business.

The Meta-Goertek relationship is now similar to joint design manufacturing, under which Meta tells Goertek its goals and requirements for its devices, Goertek proposes multiple options, and Meta chooses from them, the report said.

If it all goes well, Goertek could one day take over the design of the AR glasses, the Goertek employee reportedly said. In the new relationship, Goertek handles more of the electrical engineering for Meta’s devices, including tasks such as how to connect chips with sensors within a device.

Goertek has also started designing the outer shell for future versions of Meta’s mixed reality headsets, according to one of the Meta employees. It is also playing a larger role in research and development for other Meta devices such as the Ray-Ban smart glasses.

The report also said some of the recently laid-off Meta employees, working on hardware in Shanghai and Shenzhen, were being recruited by Goertek.

Some Meta contractors, including those involved in testing and manufacturing, are also shifting to Goertek, according to one of the Meta employees.

data.title: Microsoft AI revenue mix likely has 'very healthy' margins: Bernstein
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) was one of the early beneficiaries of the artificial intelligence spending craze. But with the stock underperforming its peers and the broader indexes in 2024, research firm Bernstein Societe Generale said it is "getting more constructive" on the tech giant, pointing to what are believed to be "very healthy" AI-related margins.

Microsoft's AI revenue comes from its software-as-a-service Copilots (excluding GitHub Copilot, which is part of Azure) and Azure AI, Bernstein analyst Mark Moerdler said. "We break the numbers apart and find that the revenue will be more stable than most would expect and less exposed to startups training [large language models]," Moerdler wrote in a note to clients. "The mix suggests that margins should also be very healthy for Microsoft AI (and not a drag to Microsoft margins)."

"With Microsoft’s stock having gone nowhere for months we are getting much more constructive on the setup and believe that within less than a year investor sentiment will swing back toward - you have to own Microsoft," he continued.

Moerdler, who has an Outperform rating and $511 price target on Microsoft, believes Office 365 at roughly one year has reached an adoption rate of between 0.7% and 1% and has an annual revenue run rate between $1B and $1.5B. This would it one of the fastest enterprise solutions to hit the $1B mark, he said.

"While smaller than most hoped by the point we believe that it will continue to grow and grow at a healthy rate for many years to come," he wrote.

AI revenue breakdown

The company said previously that its AI revenue run rate was greater than $10B as it exited the fiscal second quarter of 2025.

The largest chunk of that is Azure AI, believed to be roughly $9.7B. Included in that is Azure OpenAI, believed to have a revenue run rate between $5.1B and $5.8B.

There are also OpenAI's fees to Microsoft for inferencing. Based off media reports that that OpenAI could generate $3.7B in revenue this year (including $2.7B from ChatGPT) and that it's inferencing cost for 2024 was around $2B, Microsoft could be generating around $2.5B to $3B in inferencing revenue in fiscal 2025, Moerdler said.

And lastly, GitHub Copilot is estimated to have a $1B to $1.1B run rate business next quarter, Moerdler said.

"The bottom line here is that MSFT’s has essentially provided a ceiling for potential ‘paper’ losses from OpenAI," Moerdler wrote. "More importantly, it gives investors confidence that MSFT has not provided a blank check to OpenAI. And, any future spending on OpenAI could be monitored [sic] through [a] public funding round."

data.title: Qualigen Therapeutics regains compliance with Nasdaq's listing requirements
data.description:
• Qualigen Therapeutics (NASDAQ:QLGN) received formal notice from Nasdaq that the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement and stockholders’ equity requirement.
  • The company will be subject to a mandatory panel monitor in connection with the equity requirement for a period of one year from December 2, 2024.
  • QLGN shares were down 4.7% on Thursday.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Damon names Dominique Kwong as interim CEO
data.description:
• Damon (NASDAQ:DMN) announced the appointment of Damon co-founder, Dominique Kwong as interim chief executive officer, and Shashi Tripathi as chairman of the board.
  • Kwong assumes the interim CEO role after first serving on the company's board and as chief technology officer and then subsequently its chief operating officer between 2017 and 2023. 
  • Kwong replaces Jay Giraud as CEO.
  • DMN shares were down 2.9% on Thursday. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nano Labs enters strategic Partnership with HashKey Group for Web3 innovation
data.description:
• Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) has entered into a strategic cooperate agreement to explore in-depth cooperation in the Web3 space and blockchain ecosystem with HashKey Group.
  • This partnership marks a significant step in Nano Labs's commitment to advancing the technical innovation and application in Web3 and blockchain ecosystem.
  • Pursuant to the Agreement, a subsidiary of Nano Labs will open a trading account on HashKey Exchange, a licensed virtual asset trading platform in Hong Kong under HashKey Group, and invest in virtual assets to diversify its portfolio in the digital currency sector.
  • The collaboration extends beyond digital asset trading and is expected to include Web3 technology development and blockchain ecosystem construction, fostering innovation and growth in the Web3 industry.

data.title: Elevance under scrutiny over anesthesia coverage change for some Anthem plans
data.description:
Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) has drawn the ire of a major physicians group and the public over a policy change slated to go into effect on Feb. 1 for Anthem members in New York, Connecticut, and Missouri that would limit the amount of anethesia and anesthetic services covered during surgeries and procedures.

According to an Anthem provider newsletter, the insurer will now use the CMS Physician Work Time values as the basis to determine appropriate anesthesia coverage. "Claims submitted with reported time above the established number of minutes will be denied." However, individuals under 22 and maternity-related care are exempt from the new policy.

The American Society of Anesthesiologists is opposed to the change and sent a letter to Elevance CEO Gail Boudreau in November requesting a meeting.

"Not only is the use of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services Physician Work Time Value not an accepted, reliable, or rational method for payment, it demonstrates Anthem’s lack of knowledge regarding how anesthesia payments are made," the letter reads. "The CMS Physician Work Time was not developed nor intended to support reporting or payment for anesthesia services."

Calling the move a "cynical money grab by Anthem," ASA President Donald Arnold said in a news release, “This is just the latest in a long line of appalling behavior by commercial health insurers looking to drive their profits up at the expense of patients and physicians providing essential care."

Seeking Alpha has reached out to Elevance Health for comment but has yet to hear back.

data.title: American Eagle Outfitters downgraded at J.P. Morgan as markdowns presents a 'notable risk'
data.description:
American Eagle Outfitters (NYSE:AEO) limped into Thursday’s open with a double-digit loss as the retailer’s drop in sales and lowered full year outlook led J.P. Morgan to downgrade the stock to Neutral from Overweight.

Shares were down 16% at the open and wiped-out last week’s gains.

The reversal in the stock was triggered by disappointing Q3 results which showed a 1% drop in sales, slowing comparable sales into its Aerie and American Eagle businesses, and lowered sales guidance for the year.

“While long-term, we see AEO driving growth across the brand portfolio across both AE and Aerie and operating margin expansion on fixed expense leverage, we see recent moderating sales and consumer ‘choppiness’ experienced over the trailing 6 months contributing to inconsistent results and execution risk with markdowns a notable potential risk,” J.P. Morgan’s Matthew Boss said. Boss also cut his target price by 15% to $23, a 12% upside to Wednesday’s closing price.

The company’s creative director acknowledge the “choppiness” in non-peak periods and less consistency in its direct business during the company’s earnings call to explain the revision to Q4 comparable sales outlook.

"Moving into Holiday, we're seeing a little bit of some choppiness just in a couple of weeks, we just want to be really cautious, and we want to deliver strong results," said Jennifer Foyle.

Decelerating same store sales is a “red flag” for Boss as he sees management’s expectations for sequential comparable sales improvement problematic considering December comps coming in 200 basis points “tougher” relative to November.

Boss also lowered his adjusted earnings estimates for FY24 and FY25 to $1.66 and $1.87 from $1.78 and $2.05, respectively, versus the consensus estimates of $1.68 for FY25 and $1.89 for FY26.

The selling pressure on American Eagle (AEO) is spilling over into comparable retailers with Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) down 5%, Gap (GAP) lower by 1.6%, and shares of Urban Outfitters (URBN) down 2.5%.
